note: Cross-scale check (2026-07-24): does the weak diff-of-means WRITE negative hold at 4B? Captured from the run stderr; results.json was not written because the CONDITIONAL/probe arm crashed (the hello_world probe is trained on 1B's 1152-dim activations, the 4B model is 2560-dim). The UNCONDITIONAL diff-of-means sweep below is the WRITE test and completed cleanly.
verdict: NEGATIVE HOLDS AT 4B. Mean-pooled diff-of-means does not install refusal at 4B (refusal 0.03->0.00 as alpha rises) and only drives gibberish (0.23->0.97), the same failure pattern as 1B. Scale-robust, not a 1B-capacity artifact.
caveats: The 4B 'Extreme' build is more heavily abliterated (baseline refusal 0.03 vs 1B 0.33) -> almost no refusal circuit to restore; not a clean refusal-restoration substrate. A less-abliterated 4B (or gated base gemma-3-4b-it) would be a cleaner scale test.
layer 12 of 34 (4B) is relatively earlier than layer 12 of 26 (1B); a proportional layer would be a fairer depth match.
The conditional/probe arm needs a probe retrained on 4B activations (dim 2560).
